title_en: Salah in Radd al-Muhtar: Advanced Prayer Law
content_en:
Prayer law in Radd al-Muhtar is treated with Ibn 'Abidin's characteristic thoroughness, engaging every significant question that had arisen in the Hanafi school over the centuries and resolving them with reference to the full range of transmitted opinions, evidential analysis, and the requirements of Ottoman-era practice.

Ibn 'Abidin's discussion of prayer times gives particular attention to practical questions that arise in different geographic contexts. He discusses the hadith evidence for each prayer time and engages with the question — highly relevant in his era — of how to determine prayer times in regions with very long or very short days, foreshadowing the contemporary juristic debates on high-latitude prayer times. His methodology of returning to the underlying purpose (maqsad) of each time ruling when direct application becomes impossible is a model of classical juristic reasoning.

The Hanafi school's treatment of the imam-follower relationship is elaborated with precision. One crucial question concerns the correctness of following an imam in prayer: if the imam's prayer is invalidated by something the congregation did not know (such as the imam being in a state of ritual impurity), does this invalidate the congregation's prayer? The Hanafi school says no — the congregation's prayer remains valid. Ibn 'Abidin grounds this in principles of individual accountability and the Prophet's statement encouraging congregation even in difficult circumstances.

The question of praying behind imams of different legal schools was a live issue in Ibn 'Abidin's time given the Ottoman practice of appointing official imams of various madhabs in different mosques. Ibn 'Abidin affirms the Hanafi position that praying behind an imam of another school is valid, provided that the imam's prayer is valid according to his own school's criteria. However, he notes that if the imam performs an act that would invalidate the prayer according to the Hanafi school (such as laughing loudly in prayer), the Hanafi follower must exit the prayer.

Sujud as-sahw (prostration of forgetfulness) receives detailed treatment. Ibn 'Abidin explains the Hanafi rules precisely: it is required whenever a wajib (obligation) has been omitted or delayed forgetfully, or whenever a non-prayer action has been added forgetfully. The method is to perform two additional prostrations after the final tashahhud but before the salam — differing from the Shafi'i and Hanbali practice of performing them after the salam.

The prayers of fear (salat al-khawf) and the prayer of the sick are discussed with sensitivity to their exceptional circumstances. Ibn 'Abidin is careful to distinguish between what is an adaptation of prayer permitted by the Shari'ah (such as praying while seated due to genuine inability to stand) and what would constitute an impermissible alteration of the prayer's essential structure. The principle of maslahah (public interest) and darurah (necessity) are carefully applied without being used as blank checks.

Optional prayers (nawafil) and their role in completing deficiencies in the obligatory prayers are emphasized. The Prophet's hadith — 'The first thing the servant will be held accountable for is prayer; if it is sound, the rest of his deeds will be sound; if it is corrupt, the rest will be corrupt. If something is missing from his obligatory prayer, the Lord will say: See if My servant has any voluntary prayers' (Abu Dawud, at-Tirmidhi) — is cited to illustrate the spiritual function of nafil prayer.
content_id:
Hukum shalat dalam Radd al-Muhtar diperlakukan dengan kelengkapan khas Ibn Abidin, menangani setiap pertanyaan signifikan yang telah muncul dalam madzhab Hanafi selama berabad-abad dan menyelesaikannya dengan mengacu pada sumber-sumber utama, pandangan-pandangan yang bersaing, dan penilaian Ibn Abidin sendiri tentang posisi yang paling dapat diandalkan. Jumlah total ruang yang dicurahkan untuk hukum shalat dalam Radd al-Muhtar jauh melampaui perlakuan tersebut dalam karya-karya referensi Hanafi yang lebih awal.

Ibn Abidin memberikan perhatian khusus pada pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang muncul dari praktik ibadah di Kekaisaran Ottoman, termasuk pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang shalat di masjid-masjid yang ditransformasikan dari gereja-gereja, persyaratan untuk berbagai jenis shalat yang dipraktikkan di wilayah-wilayah Ottoman yang berbeda, dan cara menangani situasi-situasi di mana kesempatan mengikuti para pemimpin shalat dari madzhab yang berbeda muncul. Keterlibatan praktis ini membedakan Radd al-Muhtar dari karya-karya yang lebih abstrak dalam kurikulum Hanafi.

Perlakuan terhadap shalat Jumat dan shalat berjamaah sangat rinci, mencerminkan pentingnya lembaga-lembaga ini bagi kehidupan komunal Muslim dan kompleksitas pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang mereka timbulkan tentang persyaratan, kondisi, dan konsekuensi dari berbagai ketidakhadiran atau ketidaksempurnaan. Analisis Ibn Abidin tentang pertanyaan-pertanyaan ini tetap menjadi referensi standar bagi para pejabat agama Hanafi dan pengkhotbah Jumat dalam komunitas-komunitas yang mengikuti madzhab Hanafi di seluruh dunia.
title_id: Shalat dalam Radd al-Muhtar: Hukum Shalat Tingkat Lanjut
